text.div:
Generated Narrative: CodeSystem nasa-mrid-discipline-cs

This case-sensitive code system https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-mrid-discipline-cs defines the following codes:

CodeDisplayDefinition
radiation RadiationRadiation monitoring, dosimetry, and protection.
cardiovascular CardiovascularCardiovascular assessment including ECG, stress testing, and blood pressure monitoring.
bone-muscle-exercise Bone, Muscle & ExerciseMusculoskeletal health, bone density, and exercise countermeasures.
behavioral Behavior and PerformancePsychological assessment, behavioral health, and cognitive performance.
environmental Environmental HealthEnvironmental monitoring including air quality, water quality, and microbiology.
neurovestibular NeurovestibularBalance, vestibular function, and spatial orientation assessment.
ophthalmology OphthalmologyEye examinations, visual acuity, and SANS monitoring.
audiology AudiologyHearing assessment and acoustic protection.
dental DentalDental examinations and oral health.
laboratory Laboratory MedicineClinical laboratory testing and screening.
physical-examination Physical ExaminationComprehensive physical examinations and health status evaluations.
eva-medical EVA MedicalMedical monitoring and support for extravehicular activities.
reconditioning ReconditioningPost-flight rehabilitation and reconditioning.

name: NASAMRIDDisciplineCS
title: NASA MRID Medical Disciplines
description: Medical disciplines categorizing NASA Medical Requirements Integration Documents.

concept.definition: Cardiovascular assessment including ECG, stress testing, and blood pressure monitoring.
Musculoskeletal health, bone density, and exercise countermeasures.
Psychological assessment, behavioral health, and cognitive performance.
Environmental monitoring including air quality, water quality, and microbiology.
Balance, vestibular function, and spatial orientation assessment.
Eye examinations, visual acuity, and SANS monitoring.
Comprehensive physical examinations and health status evaluations.
Medical monitoring and support for extravehicular activities.
